alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim that CNN-based detection is promising but reports it with lower certainty, so the claim content is unchanged while confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Enhancing the classical VGG network with a convolutional COVID block resulted in a diagnosis performance accuracy of 98.52% for two classes and 95.34% for three classes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The enhanced VGG network achieved an overall accuracy of 98.52% for diagnosing two classes and 95.34% for three classes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same numeric performance results for the enhanced VGG model unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using the enhanced VGG deep neural network can assist radiologists in automatically diagnosing COVID-19 through radiography. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An efficient screening model based on the enhanced classical VGG network can diagnose and distinguish patients with COVID-19 from those with pneumonia and healthy individuals through radiography. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the practical utility as an explicit screening/diagnostic capability distinguishing multiple classes rather than solely 'assisting radiologists', which clarifies and broadens the claimed application.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper addresses the question of how well a grayscale digital image can be stored at a compression ratio of $n:1$ when $n>>50$. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper addresses the question of how well a grayscale image can be stored at a compression ratio of n:1 when n is significantly greater than 50. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution about storing grayscale images at very high compression ratios with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study focuses on extreme cases of image compression using '$\mathbf{V}$-variable image compression'. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study focuses on extreme cases of image compression using 'V-variable image compression'. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same focus on V-variable image compression for extreme compression cases without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper reviews new factor estimations based on modern techniques for recovering low-rank structures in high-dimensional models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article reviews new factor estimations based on modern techniques for recovering low-rank structures of high-dimensional models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same review claim about factor estimation and low-rank recovery techniques with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The survey discusses statistical inferences of several factor-augmented models and their applications in econometric learning models. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article discusses statistical inferences of several factor-augmented models and their applications in statistical learning models. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication alters the application domain wording from 'econometric learning' to the broader 'statistical learning' and lowers stated certainty, which clarifies and broadens the claim while tempering confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The final part of the survey summarizes new developments in dealing with unbalanced panels from the matrix completion perspective. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article summarizes new developments dealing with unbalanced panels from the matrix completion perspective. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the summarized developments on unbalanced panels and matrix completion but downgrades the certainty from high to medium, so the content is unchanged with tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Quantum machine learning models can offer speedups and better predictive accuracy compared to classical models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantum machine learning models can offer speedups and better predictive accuracy compared to classical models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim verbatim with equal certainty and scope, so no change is observed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Quantum algorithms are vulnerable to input perturbations, particularly in classification problems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantum algorithms are vulnerable to input perturbations, particularly in classification problems. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same vulnerability claim with equivalent scope and strength; only the label 'result' vs 'conclusion' differs but the scientific content is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a fundamental link between binary quantum hypothesis testing and provably robust quantum classification. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a fundamental link between binary quantum hypothesis testing and provably robust quantum classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same technical claim about the link between hypothesis testing and robust classification with no change in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A tight robustness condition constrains the amount of noise a classifier can tolerate, regardless of the noise source. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A tight robustness condition constrains the amount of noise a classifier can tolerate, regardless of the noise source. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The robustness condition is reported identically in the publication, preserving semantic content, scope, and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed framework allows for the study of the reliability of quantum classification protocols beyond adversarial noise scenarios. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed framework allows for the study of the reliability of quantum classification protocols beyond adversarial noise scenarios. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed attentional aggregation formulation achieves competitive performance on standard benchmarks including CUHK03, Market-1501, and DukeMTMC. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The simple method improves the performance of baseline models and achieves comparable performance with the latest advanced re-ranking methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The simple method improves the performance of baseline models and achieves comparable performance with the latest advanced re-ranking methods. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method is flexible enough to incorporate different representations and similarity metrics. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Utilizing stronger representations and metrics leads to state-of-the-art person re-ID performance, validating the general applicability of the proposed method. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed 3D U-Net achieved a median Dice score of 0.77 in the test set for 2D segmentation of breast cancers. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SVM models embedded with the random projection algorithm improve the performance of machine learning models for medical images. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study investigates the feasibility of applying a random projection algorithm to build an optimal feature vector from a large feature pool generated by CAD. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Country-specific music listening profiles are significantly influenced by the cultural and socio-economic background of the listener. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The article identifies country profiles of music preferences at the fine-grained level of music tracks, which differs from previous work that focused on the artist level. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model using projected entangled pair states (PEPS) is significantly superior to existing models using tree-like tensor networks for image classification. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model using projected entangled pair states (PEPS) is significantly superior to existing models using treelike tensor networks. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method performs as well as the multilayer perceptron classifier while using much fewer parameters and being more stable. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results indicate potential applications of two-dimensional tensor network models in machine learning. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results indicate potential applications of two-dimensional tensor network models in machine learning. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is carried over verbatim from preprint to publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed DNN model can predict high-quality annual panoramic luminance maps from limited point-in-time imagery. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model can predict high-quality annual panoramic luminance maps from a limited amount of point-in-time high-dynamic-range imagery. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The most efficient predictions of annual luminance maps are achieved with 9 days of hourly data collected around the spring equinox, summer and winter solstices. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nine days of hourly data collected around the spring equinox, summer and winter solstices can also suffice to predict annual luminance maps. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not include an explicit statement about validation against Radiance (RPICT) renderings, so this validation claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This machine learning approach makes annual luminance-based evaluations more efficient and accessible for practitioners and researchers. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithms solve stochastic smooth (strongly) convex-concave saddle-point problems using zeroth-order oracles. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The convergence rate for the stochastic term is only by a log n factor worse than for the first-order methods when the feasible set is a direct product of two simplices. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our analysis shows that the convergence rate for the stochastic term is only by a logn factor worse than for the first-order methods when the feasible set is a direct product of two simplices. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper develops 1/2th-order methods that utilize a zeroth-order oracle for minimization and a first-order oracle for maximization. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: No corresponding claim about 1/2th-order methods appears in the publication's listed claims, so this development appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The practical performance of the proposed zeroth-order and 1/2th-order methods is demonstrated on practical problems. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We compute velocities and accelerations of a one-dimensional statistical model using the canonical dual pair of parallel transports. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We compute velocities and accelerations of a one-dimensional statistical model using the canonical dual pair of parallel transports. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieves a sensitivity of 64% and a specificity of 95% in predicting IPV. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset used for training the predictive algorithms includes 34,642 radiology reports and 1479 patients of IPV victims and control patients. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset used for training the predictive algorithms includes 34,642 radiology reports and 1479 patients of IPV victims and control patients. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper presents a closed-loop motor controller implemented on mixed-signal analog-digital neuromorphic hardware using a spiking neural network.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The spiking neural network performs a proportional control action by encoding target, feedback, and error signals.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The optimization of the network structure leads to better control performances by making it more robust to noisy inputs and device mismatch.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The evaluation of novel datasets updated from the obsolete KDD99 set shows improved performance in intrusion detection.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental analyses reveal trade-offs between resource consumption and performance in neural network-based intrusion detection systems.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The best BLEU scores achieved by the model are 73.39 for the training set and 24.51 for the validation set using EfficientNetB0.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The network includes a gating mechanism that selects the most relevant input at each inference time step.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PANDA demonstrates better accuracy than existing methods over the same validation set and on an external independent test dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The IDA technique allows for more precise control of the object's position and size while preserving background information. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The background choice in the IDA method is based on inter-image optimization, while object size follows a uniform random distribution. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm shows asymptotic feasibility of the affine constraint Ax = b.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper demonstrates convergence of the Lagrangian to an optimum and asymptotic feasibility of the affine constraint in an almost sure sense.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ICGALP algorithm demonstrates convergence of the Lagrangian values in the Bregman sense.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the convergence statement to convergence of Lagrangian values in the Bregman sense, which changes the precise mathematical characterization of convergence from the preprint's original phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Weak convergence of the dual variable to a solution of the dual problem is shown in an almost sure sense.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Strong convergence of the sequence of dual variables to a solution of the dual problem is achieved in an almost sure sense.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the convergence result from weak to strong convergence of the dual variables, increasing the claim's confidence/strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical experiments confirm the predicted rates of convergence for the Lagrangian values and the feasibility gap.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical experiments verify the predicted rates of convergence.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports numerical verification of the predicted convergence rates, matching the preprint's experimental claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Almost sure convergence rates are provided for the Lagrangian values and the feasibility gap for the ergodic primal variables.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a specific theoretical claim about almost sure convergence rates for ergodic primal variables that was not stated in the preprint.

alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same cautious conclusion about potential in practical settings, with synonymous wording ('difficult to obtain' → 'intractable to capture').

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Traditional methods for feature selection are not suitable for uplift models due to differences in target objectives. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Traditional methods for feature selection are not suitable for uplift models due to differences in target objectives. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim about the mismatch between traditional feature selection and uplift objectives is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed feature selection methods explicitly designed for uplift modeling demonstrate advantages over traditional feature selection methods. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The manifold proximal point algorithm (ManPPA) converges at a sublinear rate.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ManPPA can achieve a quadratic convergence rate when applied to orthogonal dictionary learning (ODL) and robust subspace recovery (RSR) problems.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The stochastic variant of ManPPA, called StManPPA, has a sublinear convergence rate and is well suited for large-scale computation.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state the sublinear convergence rate or the large-scale suitability for StManPPA explicitly, so this specific combined claim appears absent in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both ManPPA and StManPPA have provably faster convergence rates than existing subgradient-type methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both ManPPA and StManPPA have provably faster convergence rates than existing subgradient-type methods.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The results from extensive numerical experiments demonstrate the efficiency and efficacy of the proposed methods.

alignments.rationale: This publication claim about a semismooth Newton-based ALM and its superlinear rate does not correspond to any preprint claim and is therefore an added result in the final paper.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel approach to construct priors based on the Maximum Entropy (MaxEnt) principle is introduced. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments on a variety of real-world networks confirm that CSNE outperforms the state-of-the-art on the task of sign prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CSNE outperforms the state-of-the-art on the task of sign prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts the same empirical superiority on sign prediction as reported in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MaxEnt priors achieve accuracies competitive with the state-of-the-art at very limited computational cost, providing an excellent runtime-accuracy trade-off. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The MaxEnt priors achieve accuracies competitive with the state-of-the-art at very limited computational cost. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the claim about competitive accuracy and low computational cost for MaxEnt priors with equivalent framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Fast SeqProp algorithm achieves up to 100-fold faster convergence compared to previous versions of activation maximization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fast SeqProp results in up to 100-fold faster convergence compared to prior methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same empirical speedup claim with equivalent language and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fast SeqProp finds improved fitness optima for many applications. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fast SeqProp finds improved fitness optima for many applications. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim about improved optima across applications with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of an adaptive entropy variable in Fast SeqProp removes bottlenecks arising from overly large or skewed sampling parameters. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fast SeqProp offers a reliable and efficient method for general-purpose sequence optimization through a differentiable fitness predictor. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes mechanism-level detail about adaptive entropy as a broader claim about reliability and efficiency via a differentiable predictor, increasing the generality and certainty of the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fast SeqProp is demonstrated by designing DNA and protein sequences for six deep learning predictors, including a protein structure predictor. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not repeat the specific demonstration details (six predictors, protein structure predictor); instead it generalizes potential applications, so the concrete demonstration claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine Learning technology can be applied to improve mathematical software by optimizing choices that impact performance without affecting mathematical correctness. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine Learning technology can be applied to improve mathematical software without compromising its mathematical correctness. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core conclusion but expresses it with greater certainty (higher stated confidence).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The variable ordering used when building a Cylindrical Algebraic Decomposition (CAD) can be optimized using Machine Learning models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A software pipeline was created to utilize sklearn for selecting variable ordering in algorithms that act on polynomial systems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication demonstrates the ML-based optimization in practice via a sklearn pipeline for selecting variable ordering, thus clarifying and concretely supporting the optimization claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: New techniques for feature generation and hyper-parameter selection were developed and can be adapted for decisions beyond CAD variable ordering. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: New techniques for feature generation and hyper-parameter selection were developed and can be adapted for other decision-making processes. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution with near-identical wording and intended generality.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A software pipeline has been created to utilize sklearn for selecting variable ordering in algorithms that act on polynomial systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A software pipeline was created to utilize sklearn for selecting variable ordering in algorithms that act on polynomial systems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same software pipeline contribution with equivalent language and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CLaSS is an efficient computational method for attribute-controlled generation of molecules.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of deep learning and molecular dynamics may accelerate the discovery of potent and selective broad-spectrum antimicrobials.
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserts a specific method (CLaSS) for attribute-controlled molecule generation, whereas the publication only makes a broader, more tentative statement about DL+MD accelerating antimicrobial discovery; the method name/claim is generalized and softened.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach successfully designed non-toxic antimicrobial peptides (AMPs) with strong broad-spectrum potency.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified, synthesized and experimentally tested 20 candidate antimicrobial peptides, of which two displayed high potency against diverse Gram-positive and Gram-negative pathogens.
alignments.rationale: The publication confirms the key result (designed peptides with broad-spectrum potency) but reports it in the specific experimental context (two potent peptides out of 20), clarifying the empirical support.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Synthesis and testing of twenty designed sequences identified two novel AMPs with high potency against diverse pathogens.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified, synthesized and experimentally tested 20 candidate antimicrobial peptides, of which two displayed high potency against diverse Gram-positive and Gram-negative pathogens.
alignments.rationale: This publication claim restates the preprint result essentially verbatim: 20 synthesized/tested candidates and two high-potency peptides across diverse pathogens.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both identified antimicrobials exhibit low in vitro and in vivo toxicity and mitigate the onset of drug resistance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both peptides have low toxicity, as validated in vitro and in mice.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly confirms the low in vitro and in vivo toxicity, but the preprint's combined claim about also mitigating drug resistance is not fully preserved here (resistance is treated separately in the publication), so the toxicity part is retained while the overall claim is narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed statistical model can be used to detect precisely the mu-suppression from EEG signals.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed statistical model can be used to detect mu-suppression from EEG signals.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint result with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generalized extreme value distribution (GEV) is effective in representing the power spectrum density of the EEG signal in the central motor cortex.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generalized extreme value distribution (GEV) effectively represents the power spectrum density of the EEG signal in the central motor cortex.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim about GEV is restated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The designed linear classifier achieves very good classification accuracy in distinguishing between imagery, movement, and resting EEG events.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The designed linear classifier achieves very good classification accuracy in distinguishing between imagery, movement, and resting EEG events.
alignments.rationale: The performance claim about the linear classifier is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The FedAvg algorithm can behave erratically for non-convex problems without strong and unrealistic assumptions on the problem structure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The FedAvg algorithm can behave erratically without strong and unrealistic assumptions on the problem structure.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core warning about FedAvg's erratic behavior but omits the explicit 'non-convex' qualifier, slightly rephrasing the claim while keeping its intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new algorithm design strategy from the primal-dual optimization perspective is proposed to design FL algorithms that are provably fast and require as few assumptions as possible.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm design strategy from the primal-dual optimization perspective yields algorithms that can deal with non-convex objective functions.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the primal-dual design perspective and its ability to handle non-convex objectives, matching the preprint contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithms achieve the best possible optimization and communication complexity while being able to deal with both the full batch and mini-batch local computation models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithms achieve the best possible optimization and communication complexity in a well-defined sense.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim about optimal complexity but frames it more precisely ('in a well-defined sense'); the part about full/mini-batch is addressed elsewhere in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithms are communication efficient, with an adaptive communication pattern based on the level of heterogeneity among the local data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The communication effort of the proposed algorithms can be reduced when the level of heterogeneity among the local data decreases.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same communication-efficiency property, linking communication effort to data heterogeneity as in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This is the first algorithmic framework for FL that achieves all the above properties.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This is the first algorithmic framework for Federated Learning that achieves communication efficiency while accommodating full-batch and mini-batch local computation models.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the 'first framework' claim but restates it with a narrower focus (communication efficiency plus accommodation of computation models) rather than claiming all previously listed properties broadly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The design of optical networks using multiple diffractive layers can transform and encode spatial information into the power spectrum of diffracted light for optical classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Optical networks composed of diffractive layers trained using deep learning can encode the spatial information of objects into the power spectrum of the diffracted light.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the core result that trained diffractive-layer optical networks encode spatial information into the diffracted-light power spectrum.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine vision framework was experimentally validated to classify images of handwritten digits using a plasmonic nanoantenna-based detector at terahertz spectrum.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The single-pixel machine vision framework was experimentally validated to classify images of handwritten digits using a plasmonic nanoantenna-based detector.
alignments.rationale: The experimental validation claim for digit classification with the plasmonic detector is preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The coupling of spectral encoding through a diffractive optical network with a shallow electronic neural network allows for the reconstruction of images based solely on spectral information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A shallow electronic neural network was trained to rapidly reconstruct images of handwritten digits based solely on the spectral power detected at ten distinct wavelengths.
alignments.rationale: The publication confirms the reconstruction-from-spectral-power claim and provides greater specificity (ten wavelengths) and higher certainty, amplifying the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reconstructed images can be cycled back as new inputs to the diffractive network to enhance optical object classification.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or emphasize the iterative 'cycle back' concept for improving classification; this specific claim is not present in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This machine vision framework can be extended to other spectral-domain measurement systems for new 3D imaging and sensing modalities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The single-pixel machine vision framework can be extended to other spectral-domain measurement systems for new 3D imaging and sensing modalities.
alignments.rationale: Both versions make the same forward-looking claim about extending the framework to other spectral-domain systems and 3D imaging/sensing modalities.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An open-retrieval conversational question answering (ORConvQA) setting is introduced to improve conversational search systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of the open-retrieval conversational question answering (ORConvQA) setting addresses the limitations of previous approaches to conversational search.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the contribution of introducing ORConvQA and frames it as addressing prior limitations, matching the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A learnable retriever is crucial for the effectiveness of ORConvQA.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A learnable retriever is crucial for the effectiveness of ORConvQA.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the experimental/result claim about the importance of a learnable retriever unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Enabling history modeling in all system components leads to substantial improvements in the ORConvQA system.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Enabling history modeling in all system components leads to substantial improvements in the ORConvQA system's performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the preprint's claim that history modeling across components improves performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reranker component provides a regularization effect that contributes to the model performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The reranker component provides a regularization effect that contributes to the overall model performance.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the reranker's regularization effect on overall performance, matching the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method enables unsupervised learning of equations of motion from raw and distorted unlabeled video.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method presented enables unsupervised learning of equations of motion for objects in raw and distorted unlabeled synthetic video.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution but adds the qualifier "synthetic video," narrowing the scope; the core claim (unsupervised learning from raw/distorted unlabeled video) is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The pre-regression step can rediscover Cartesian coordinates of unlabeled moving objects despite video distortion.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The pre-regression step is capable of rediscovering Cartesian coordinates of unlabeled moving objects even in the presence of video distortion.
alignments.rationale: Wording is effectively identical and the meaning is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adding extra latent space dimensions facilitates the pregression step by avoiding topological problems during training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The addition of extra latent space dimensions facilitates the pregression step by avoiding topological problems during training.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion with equivalent wording and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method employs Pareto-optimal symbolic regression to discover differential equations describing motion.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate the claim about Pareto-optimal symbolic regression; instead it introduces a different result (autodiscovery of an inertial frame), so this preprint claim appears omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: An inertial frame is autodiscovered by minimizing the combined equation complexity across multiple experiments.
alignments.rationale: This publication claim (autodiscovery of an inertial frame via combined equation complexity) has no counterpart in the preprint and appears as a newly reported result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing approaches to solving combinatorial optimization problems on graphs require algorithmic engineering for each problem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing approaches to solving combinatorial optimization problems on graphs require algorithmic engineering for each problem.
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over verbatim from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The practical side of theoretical computer science, including computational complexity, needs to be addressed in the context of combinatorial optimization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The practical side of theoretical computer science, including computational complexity, needs to be addressed in the context of combinatorial optimization.
alignments.rationale: Identical statement and intent in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Relevant developments in machine learning research on graphs are applicable to solving combinatorial optimization problems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Relevant developments in machine learning research on graphs are applicable to solving combinatorial optimization problems.
alignments.rationale: Content and emphasis remain the same between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The structures involved in learning to solve combinatorial optimization problems are organized and compared with a focus on telecommunications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The structures involved in learning to solve combinatorial optimization problems are organized and compared with a focus on telecommunications.
alignments.rationale: The review/organization claim is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models are vulnerable to nontargeted and targeted universal adversarial perturbations (UAPs), even in the case of small UAPs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models are vulnerable to non-targeted and targeted universal adversarial perturbations (UAPs), even in the case of small UAPs.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the vulnerability claim with equivalent meaning and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 2% norm of the UAPs to the average norm of an image in the dataset achieves >85% and >90% success rates for the nontargeted and targeted attacks, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 2% norm of the UAPs to the average norm of an image achieves >85% and >90% success rates for non-targeted and targeted attacks, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The numerical attack-success claim is reproduced exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Due to the nontargeted UAPs, the DNN models judge most chest X-ray images as COVID-19 cases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DNN models judge most chest X-ray images as COVID-19 cases due to non-targeted UAPs.
alignments.rationale: The content is the same, but the publication reports this with reduced certainty (preprint high → publication medium), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results indicate that careful consideration is required in practical applications of DNNs to COVID-19 diagnosis.
alignments.rationale: The explicit cautionary/conclusion about practical deployment is not represented in the publication claims list, suggesting it was omitted or de-emphasized.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Iterative fine-tuning of the DNN models using UAPs improves the robustness of the DNN models against UAPs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Iterative fine-tuning of DNN models using UAPs improves the robustness of DNN models against UAPs.
alignments.rationale: The contribution of iterative fine-tuning to robustness appears verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Point2Mesh introduces a technique for reconstructing a surface mesh from an input point cloud using a self-prior defined automatically from the input data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Point2Mesh is a technique for reconstructing a surface mesh from an input point cloud using a self-prior defined automatically from the input data.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is carried unchanged from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The self-prior encapsulates reoccurring geometric repetitions from a single shape within the weights of a deep neural network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The self-prior encapsulates reoccurring geometric repetitions from a single shape within the weights of a deep neural network.
alignments.rationale: The descriptive claim about the self-prior's role is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Shrink-wrapping a point cloud with a self-prior converges to a desirable solution compared to a prescribed smoothness prior.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Shrink-wrapping a point cloud with a self-prior converges to a desirable solution compared to a prescribed smoothness prior.
alignments.rationale: Result about convergence and comparison to a smoothness prior is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Point2Mesh is robust to non-ideal conditions such as unoriented normals, noise, and missing parts, which often degrade the performance of traditional reconstruction approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Point2Mesh is robust to non-ideal conditions such as unoriented normals, noise, and missing parts.
alignments.rationale: Robustness claim is retained; publication wording is slightly shorter but the substance is equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This article presents a comprehensive review and categorization of information popularity prediction methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This article presents a comprehensive review and categorization of information popularity prediction methods.
alignments.rationale: The review/contribution statement is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The article defines different types of information cascades and summarizes the perspectives of existing studies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article defines different types of information cascades and summarizes the perspectives of existing studies.
alignments.rationale: The definitional and summary claim is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A taxonomy categorizing existing works into three main groups and their subclasses is presented.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A taxonomy categorizing existing works into three main groups and their subclasses is presented.
alignments.rationale: The taxonomy claim remains the same in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The article systematically reviews cutting-edge research work in the field of information diffusion.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article systematically reviews cutting-edge research work in the field of information diffusion.
alignments.rationale: The systematic review claim is carried over without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The article outlines the open challenges and opportunities in the field of information popularity prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article outlines the open challenges and opportunities in the field of information popularity prediction.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about open challenges and opportunities is preserved, with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm robustly extracts the most relevant features to classify different grasping tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm robustly extracts the most relevant features to classify different grasping tasks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same claim word-for-word about robust feature extraction for grasping tasks; only the label changed to 'contribution'.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm retains the natural meaning of the selected features, simplifying the recording setup. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm simplifies the recording setup to minimize data traffic over the communication network. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the simplification claim but reframes it specifically as minimizing network data traffic and drops the explicit claim about preserving 'natural meaning', so the claim is revised and more focused.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm minimizes data traffic over the communication network while providing physiologically significant features for medical interpretation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm simplifies the recording setup to minimize data traffic over the communication network. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the data-traffic minimization element but omits the assertion about providing physiologically significant features for medical interpretation, so the medical-interpretability component has been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The robustness of the algorithm is ensured by consensus clustering as a feature selection strategy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm robustly extracts the most relevant features to classify different grasping tasks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves a robustness claim about feature extraction but does not attribute robustness specifically to consensus clustering, so the mechanism claim was dropped/altered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A nested cross-validation scheme is used to evaluate the classification performance of the algorithm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention the nested cross-validation evaluation procedure present in the preprint, so this methodological claim appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our encodings achieve classification accuracies up to 82.8% and cluster purities up to 69.4% with a specified number of clusters, and 99.9% with an unspecified number of clusters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our encodings achieve classification accuracies up to 82.8% and cluster purities up to 69.4% (number of clusters equals number of classes), and 99.9% (unspecified number of clusters) respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same accuracy and purity figures with only a parenthetical clarification about cluster-to-class correspondence; the numerical result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We observe a relatively low correlation between text and math similarity, which indicates the independence of text and formulae. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We observe a relatively low correlation between text and math similarity, which indicates the independence of text and formulae and motivates treating them as separate features of a document. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit implication (treating text and math as separate features) to the observed low correlation, clarifying and extending the original observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification and clustering can be employed for document search and recommendation. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classification and clustering can be employed, e.g., for document search and recommendation. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same application claim (document search and recommendation) with identical intent and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computer outperforms a human expert when classifying documents. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The computer outperforms a human expert when classifying documents. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using our method to decide which units to train improves significantly the win rate against the second-best microRTS bot from the partially observable track. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using our method to decide which units to train improves significantly the win rate against the second-best microRTS bot from the partially observable track. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this experimental win-rate improvement claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method is resilient in chaotic environments, with a very small loss of efficiency only. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method is resilient in chaotic environments, with a very small loss of efficiency only. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The resilience claim is reproduced unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The source code of microPhantom and the Constraint Programming toolkit it uses are available to allow replicability and facilitate further research. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The source code of microPhantom and the Constraint Programming toolkit it uses are available to allow replicability and facilitate further research. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the availability and replicability claim exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GeoCoV19 is a large-scale Twitter dataset containing more than 524 million multilingual tweets posted over a period of 90 days since February 1, 2020. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: GeoCoV19 is a large-scale Twitter dataset containing more than 524 million multilingual tweets posted over a period of 90 days since February 1, 2020. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The dataset size, period, and description are repeated verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of geolocated social media data can empower research communities to evaluate how societies are coping with global crises. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The largescale, multilingual, geolocated social media data can empower research communities to evaluate how societies are coping with global crises. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds descriptors (large-scale, multilingual) to the geolocated data claim, clarifying and slightly broadening its scope while preserving the core conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset can be used to develop computational methods for identifying fake news and understanding communities' knowledge gaps. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset can help develop computational methods to address challenges such as identifying fake news and understanding communities' knowledge gaps. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same potential applications (fake news detection, understanding knowledge gaps) with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Non-traditional data sources like social media are becoming vital for disease forecasts and surveillance during epidemic and pandemic outbreaks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of non-traditional data sources like social media is becoming vital for disease forecasts and surveillance during epidemic and pandemic outbreaks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-certainty claim about the increasing importance of social media for disease forecasting and surveillance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Rdimtools is an R package that supports 133 DR and 17 IDE algorithms. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Rdimtools is an R package that supports 143 dimension reduction and manifold learning methods. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication changes the reported number of supported dimension-reduction methods (133 -> 143) and frames them as 'dimension reduction and manifold learning methods', so the numerical and framing details were revised and broadened.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Rdimtools facilitates multifaceted scrutiny of high-dimensional data in one place. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The extensive features of Rdimtools facilitate multifaceted scrutiny of high-dimensional data in one place. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds that the facilitation is due to 'extensive features', clarifying the mechanism behind the same overall conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Rdimtools is distributed under the MIT license and is accessible from CRAN, GitHub, and its package website. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not mention licensing or access locations, so this distribution/license claim from the preprint appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method improves the performance of algorithms related to parallel-beam X-ray CT by calculating the Gram filter exactly and optimally interpolating the sinogram signal. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method improves the performance of algorithms related to parallel-beam X-ray CT by calculating the Gram filter exactly and optimally interpolating the sinogram signal. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication claim are word-for-word the same contribution describing exact Gram filter calculation and optimal sinogram interpolation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model efficiently includes the detector blur effect. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model efficiently includes the detector blur effect. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in both versions about efficient inclusion of detector blur.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate improvements in speed and quality for back projection and iterative reconstruction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate improvements in speed and quality for back projection and iterative reconstruction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental result statements match exactly between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed implicit generative ensemble post-processing framework outperforms well-established model combination benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed implicit generative ensemble post-processing framework outperforms well-established model combination benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is stated identically in both preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method generates multivariate electricity price scenarios with a coherent dependency structure. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method generates multivariate electricity price scenarios with a coherent dependency structure. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantization has been proven to be an effective method for reducing the computing and/or storage cost of DNNs. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results demonstrate that our proposed quantization solution is more accurate and effective than the state-of-the-art approaches yet with more flexible bitwidth support. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed metric, Vector Loss, significantly outperforms the traditional iterative L2 loss minimization process in terms of effectiveness, quantization loss, and final DNN accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published text shifts emphasis from a broad claim about the overall solution and bitwidth flexibility to a focused claim about the Vector Loss metric outperforming L2, narrowing the original broader SOTA comparison.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of click data in learning to rank leads to unnecessary pairwise comparisons between relevant documents, hindering unbiased ranker optimization. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of click data leads to unnecessary pairwise comparisons between relevant documents, preventing unbiased ranker optimization. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The new weighting scheme, Propensity Ratio Scoring (PRS), corrects the bias in clicks and avoids relevant-relevant document comparisons in LTR training. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The new weighting scheme, Propensity Ratio Scoring (PRS), corrects the bias in clicks and avoids relevant-relevant document comparisons in LTR training. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CounterSample converges faster than traditional methods in biased LTR scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CounterSample converges faster than standard methods, as supported by extensive experimentation in biased LTR scenarios.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the claim by explicitly citing extensive experimental support and increases certainty (medium→high), clarifying evidence for the faster convergence statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experimentation demonstrates the effectiveness of CounterSample across various optimizers, batch sizes, and degrees of position bias.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CounterSample converges faster than standard methods, as supported by extensive experimentation in biased LTR scenarios.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the experimental-support claim but omits the preprint's explicit enumeration of optimizers, batch sizes and bias degrees, narrowing the scope while increasing reported confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Convergence is slow for SGD approaches, especially when there are large IPS weights.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an additional standalone statement emphasizing slow convergence with large IPS weights that was not separately listed as a distinct claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are significant instabilities in the geometry of embedding spaces independent of the centrality of a node.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Individual predictions in node classification can differ substantially due to randomness in the underlying embeddings.
alignments.rationale: The preprint focuses on geometric instabilities (including independence from centrality); the publication reframes instability as variability in individual predictions, a narrower, more application-focused statement and with somewhat reduced emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy of node classification seems to be unaffected by random seeding while the actual classification of nodes can vary significantly.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The overall accuracy of downstream tasks is mostly unaffected by the geometric instabilities in the underlying embeddings.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly emphasizes that overall accuracy is mostly unaffected (matching the preprint's accuracy point) and frames it as a broader observation about downstream tasks, with stronger certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Instability effects need to be taken into account when working with node embeddings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of downstream tasks is largely unaffected by randomness in node embeddings.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's recommendation to account for instabilities is not sustained; the publication instead concludes downstream performance is largely unaffected, representing a substantive revision of the original recommendation.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Link prediction exhibits high stability in overall accuracy and higher stability in individual predictions compared to node classification.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a new, specific finding about link prediction stability that does not appear in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method demonstrated the advantage of deep learning with 3D volumetric data over 2D depth images for force estimation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method demonstrated the advantage of deep learning with 3D volumetric data over 2D depth images for force estimation.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint result verbatim; there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using 4D spatio-temporal data outperforms all previously used data representations with a mean absolute error of 10.7mN.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using 4D spatio-temporal data outperforms all previously used data representations with a mean absolute error of 10.7 mN.
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over unchanged, including the numeric MAE figure.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Temporal information is valuable for force estimation and the feasibility of force prediction is demonstrated.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Temporal information is valuable for force estimation and the feasibility of force prediction is demonstrated.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the preprint's conclusion about temporal information and feasibility of prediction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Efficient decoupling of spatial and temporal processing is advantageous for 4D force estimation architectures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Efficient decoupling of spatial and temporal processing is advantageous for 4D force estimation architectures.
alignments.rationale: The claim about architectural design is repeated without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose FMSTS, a novel Reinforcement Learning approach specifically designed for optimising branching strategies in mixed integer linear programs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose FMSTS, a novel Reinforcement Learning approach specifically designed for optimising branching strategies in mixed integer linear programs.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the contribution claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method demonstrates a strong consistency between a local value function and a global metric of interest.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method demonstrates a strong consistency between a local value function and a global metric of interest.
alignments.rationale: The result is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Computational experiments indicate that our method is appropriate and able to generalise well to new instances.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Computational experiments indicate that our method is appropriate and able to generalise well to new instances.
alignments.rationale: The experimental generalization claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This is the first time Reinforcement Learning has been used to fully optimise the branching strategy in mixed integer linear programs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This is the first time Reinforcement Learning has been used to fully optimise the branching strategy in mixed integer linear programs.
alignments.rationale: The novelty/first-time claim is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Vapnik-Chervonenkis entropy of a kernel machine operating on data grouped into equally labelled subsets is non-monotonic in the size of the training set.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Vapnik-Chervonenkis entropy of a kernel machine operating on data grouped into equally labeled subsets is nonmonotonic in the size of the training set.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the technical result with only minor wording/formatting differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An additional critical point besides the storage capacity is observed in the entropy of the kernel machine.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An additional critical point besides the storage capacity is observed in the entropy of the kernel machine.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the observation exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Margin classifiers exhibit the same non-monotonic behavior in entropy even with randomly labelled data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Margin classifiers exhibit the same nonmonotonic behavior in entropy even with randomly labeled data.
alignments.rationale: The claim about margin classifiers is carried over unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings reveal aspects of expressivity that go beyond the storage capacity of neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings reveal aspects of expressivity that go beyond the storage capacity of neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The interpretive conclusion is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results indicate a path towards more realistic bounds for the generalization error of neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results indicate a path towards more realistic bounds for the generalization error of neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the forward-looking implication about generalization bounds without substantive change.
